description: TCT_NGC dev32 class severity tiers + cost matrix rules. Used by tools/eval_clinical_metrics.py to compute clinical screening + cost-weighted metrics. Each class is assigned to exactly one tier; 'ignored' classes are background cytology and don't participate in screening positive/negative judgment.
cost_matrix_rule_doc: Severity tier indices: L0=0 (negative), L1=1 (atypical), L2=2 (suspicious), L3=3 (malignant). 'k' is the predicted-class severity index (or true severity for misses). Multiplier 10 for false negatives encodes the clinical-conservative bias (missing a malignant cell is 10x worse than over-calling a benign one). The implementation should treat 'ignored' classes as if they don't appear in either GT or predictions for the cost calculation. Edit this file to retune for different clinical operating points (e.g. screening triage that tolerates more overcall vs. final diagnostic that minimizes overcall).
